name: calendula officinalis
mechanism: Primarily topical activity; preclinical and limited clinical evidence suggests anti-inflammatory and wound-healing effects.
contraindications: avoid if allergic to Asteraceae/Compositae plants
safetyNotes: No direct toxicity data. Contextual inference: Anti-inflammatory
antifungal. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation
nan.. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation

sources.title: Systematic review: Calendula officinalis extract for wound healing
sources.note: Review-level evidence for topical wound-healing use; certainty is limited by heterogeneous studies.

description: Traditional topical herb used for minor skin inflammation and wound support; human evidence is limited and mostly adjunctive.
dosage: Topical preparations are most common; product-specific dosing should follow labeling and clinical guidance.
legalStatus: Widely legal; approved for use in herbal products
preparation: Petals used fresh or dried in teas, infusions, salves, and tinctures
region: No direct region data. Contextual inference: Anti-inflammatory, wound healing, antifungal. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation, antioxidant effects, or enzyme inhibition..
